item.title: Kazakh Financial Watchdog Pushes Firms to Use Local Messenger
item.description: Kazakhstan’s financial watchdog is urging insurers and some financial firms to shift client communications to a homegrown messaging app, according to people familiar with the matter, part of a wider campaign to move users away from foreign alternatives.

item.title: Toronto Home Prices Slip Back to 2020 Levels as Turmoil Lingers
item.description: Home prices in Toronto fell to their lowest level in more than five years as an outlook for slow economic growth and broader global turmoil make prospective buyers cautious.

item.title: China’s Ramps Up Gold-Buying as Middle East War Dents Prices
item.description: China’s central bank bought the most gold in more than a year in March, demonstrating that a key pillar of support for the precious metal remains intact as prices come under pressure amid the Iran war.

item.title: UK Private Sector Flatlines in March as Stagflation Fears Rise
item.description: Britain’s private sector growth ground to a halt in the first month of the Iran war, according to a survey that pointed to a sudden loss in momentum and the threat of “stagflation.”

item.title: Iran Keeps Up Attacks Before US Deadline, Dimming Peace Chances
item.description: Iran pressed on with attacks across the Persian Gulf hours before US President Donald Trump’s deadline to agree to a ceasefire plan, clouding prospects for a breakthrough in efforts to end the war.

item.title: European Stocks Are Losing an Edge That Took Them Years to Gain
item.description: It took years to lure investors back to European stocks. Making them stay is starting to be a challenge.

item.title: Trading Ban Upends Biggest Indian Bank’s $5 Billion Rupee Short
item.description: India’s biggest bank had about $5 billion of bets against the rupee that were impacted by the regulator’s crackdown on potential speculators, according to people with knowledge of the matter, roughly 20% of the total exposure.

item.title: Traders Position for Another Trump Deadline as Frustration Grows
item.description: President Donald Trump’s latest deadline for Iran to agree to a deal is just hours away, and investors are once again finding themselves forced to prepare for a range of possible outcomes.

item.title: Germany Power Prices Turn Deeply Negative on Renewables Surge
item.description: Germany’s power prices slid on Easter Monday as a surge in renewable energy collided with unusually weak demand, sending electricity costs to deeply negative levels.

item.title: ING Scraps Agreement to Sell Its Russian Operations
item.description: ING Groep NV has ended an agreement to sell its Russian business, complicating the bank’s protracted retreat from the country.

item.title: ECB’s Elderson Presses Green-Transition Pitch After Energy Surge
item.description: Surging energy costs due to the war in the Middle East are the latest reminder for Europe to urgently reduce its reliance on fossil fuels, one of the European Central Bank’s top officials wrote in an op-ed published by the Financial Times.

item.title: Czech Inflation Stays Below Target as Policymakers Gauge Oil Hit
item.description: Czech inflation accelerated less than expected and remained below the official target as the central bank seeks to assess the broader impact of more expensive fuels on cost of living and economic growth.

item.title: LG Energy Posts Bigger-Than-Expected Loss as EV Aid Fades
item.description: LG Energy Solution Ltd. reported preliminary first-quarter earnings that missed analyst estimates, as waning electric-vehicle support in key markets like the US weighed on results, failing to offset strong demand for energy storage systems.

item.title: India Bank Options Hedging Costs Climb on RBI, Geopolitical Risk
item.description: The cost of hedging against swings in a key Indian banking gauge relative to the broader market has surged, as traders brace for the central bank’s rate decision as well as a potential escalation in the Middle East war.

item.title: Indonesia Reforms Seen Averting MSCI Cut, Not Weighting Hit
item.description: Indonesian market reforms to meet MSCI Inc.’s demands may stave off a downgrade though they don’t go far enough to prevent a lower weighting in global indexes, some analysts said.

item.title: India Returns to Buying Venezuelan Crude to Ease Supply Crunch
item.description: India is set to import the most oil from Venezuela in almost six years, helping the world’s third-largest crude importer replace Middle East grades disrupted by the Iran war.

item.title: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson to Step Down Amid Mounting Losses
item.description: Air India Chief Executive Officer Campbell Wilson has resigned, people familiar with the matter said, as the Tata Group-owned carrier confronts record losses and operational setbacks.

item.title: Dangote Says Nigerian Crude Supply to Refinery Doubled in March
item.description: Nigeria doubled crude supply to Dangote Refinery in March as Africa’s top oil producer moved to shore up fuel availability after the Iran war disrupted Middle East shipments.

item.title: Ackman’s Pershing Square Offers to Buy Universal Music Group
item.description: Pershing Square Capital Management has offered to buy Universal Music group for €9.4 billion ($10.8 billion) in cash plus shares, according to a statement on Tuesday.

item.title: Sweden’s Easing Inflation Bolsters Case for Riksbank Rate Pause
item.description: Sweden’s core inflation slowed to a four-and-a-half-year low in March, despite the impact of the war in Iran, likely reassuring policymakers to stick with an interest-rate pause until well into next year.

item.title: European Gas Ticks Higher as Trump Threatens Iran War Escalation
item.description: European natural gas prices edged higher after US President Donald Trump escalated threats to obliterate key Iranian infrastructure if his terms for a deal to end the conflict aren’t met before a Tuesday deadline.

item.title: Japan Relies on Offshore Oil Transfers as Middle East Risks Rise
item.description: Japan is relying on ship‑to‑ship oil transfers far from the Middle East to secure crude supplies while keeping its tankers out of a conflict zone that has become too risky for crews and vessels.

item.title: Singapore Unveils S$1 Billion in Measures to Address War Crisis
item.description: Singapore unveiled around S$1 billion ($778 million) in enhanced support measures to cushion households and businesses from higher energy costs triggered by the war in Iran.

item.title: Markets Struggle for Direction Ahead of Trump’s Iran Deadline
Bitcoin Slides With Risk Assets as Trump’s Iran Ultimatum Looms
item.description: Bitcoin slipped in Asia Tuesday, as cryptocurrencies were caught up in the broader market volatility ahead of President Donald Trump’s deadline for Iran.

item.title: Singapore Says US Removed ‘Inaccurate’ Trade Figure Amid Probes
item.description: Singapore said the US has withdrawn an “inaccurate statement” on bilateral trade balances that had underpinned Washington’s decision to launch probes into more than a dozen economies last month.

item.title: RBI Set to Hold Rates While Confronting Weak Rupee: Policy Guide
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India is likely to hold interest rates on Wednesday in its first policy decision since the Iran war, as it grapples with a sharply weaker rupee while trying to support economic growth.

item.title: Oil Shock Pushes Thailand’s Inflation Toward Positive Territory
item.description: Thailand’s yearlong stretch of falling prices is nearing an end, as higher oil costs and Middle East supply disruptions begin feeding through to inflation.

item.title: China’s Electric-Arc Steelmakers Regain Edge as Profits Recover
item.description: China’s electric-arc furnace steelmakers boosted weekly capacity utilization to the highest in more than two years, as the greener production method becomes more competitive.

item.title: Trumpworld’s Growing Sway in Arms Sales on Display in Rome
item.description: A summit of US, NATO and African brass featured crypto, AI and other businesses favored by the administration alongside traditional weapons vendors.
